title: Complex life on Earth may last 500 million years longer than expected
description: As the sun expands over the coming billions of years, Earth will become inhospitable to any life more complex than a microbe – but that might take longer than we thought
author: Leah Crane

title: Ancient monument marked summer solstice centuries before Stonehenge
description: Archaeologists have discovered traces of a wooden structure built 5000 years ago, 5 kilometres from Stonehenge, which appears to have been an even older monument for marking the summer solstice
author: Michael Marshall

title: Cervical cancer deaths have plummeted thanks to HPV vaccine
description: We already know the vaccine against human papillomavirus, or HPV, greatly reduces infections and cases of cervical cancer, and now we have the first evidence it prevents deaths too
author: Michael Le Page

title: Chilling the body with drugs could limit brain damage from stroke
description: Putting brain cells into a hibernation-like state via drugs that cool down core body temperature may help to preserve them following a stroke
author: Alice Klein

title: Oldest known plague outbreak killed hunter-gatherer children
description: DNA evidence shows that plague bacteria devastated a community in Siberia more than 5000 years ago, challenging the idea that there were no major disease outbreaks before the advent of farming and large settlements
author: Michael Le Page

title: Pigeons lock their eyes in place when they are flying
description: Bird-mounted headsets and backpacks have revealed the surprising things pigeons do with their eyes when on the wing
author: Chris Simms
